description: Meet Jackie, a Figma API user from the start and Plugins beta user. Check out the useful tools he has been building for Microsoft and the broader community.

title: Automate work. Bring in real data. Extend what’s possible with Figma Plugins.
description: We're excited to introduce plugins to the entire Figma community. Today, the gates are open for anyone to use plugins built by our community and build your own plugins tailored to your workflow.

title: How to run a design sprint
description: Design sprints are an effective processes used by teams to solve complex problems in a short period of time. Learn how to plan and facilitate the six phases of a design sprint.

title: The birth of Inter: How the new open-source typeface used by GitHub and Mozilla came to be
description: It takes a lot of work to invent a typeface. Figma designer Rasmus Andersson learned that first hand while creating Inter — a font meant to make it easier to read text on computer screens.

title: 5 utility plugins to speed up your workflow
description: Check out these new plugins and see how you can start working more efficiently.

title: How to wireframe
description: Learn when to create a wireframe, what goes into them, and how they can help you communicate and iterate on ideas with your team.

title: Design for everyone with these accessibility-focused plugins
description: Address accessibility challenges like color contrast and keyboard navigation with these helpful plugins.

title: How to build a plugin system on the web and also sleep well at night
description: How do you run plugins with security, stability, and performance? Our pursuit for the perfect plugin solution, and how our approach helps us run Figma plugins in a safe way.

title: Plugins to help you design with real content
description: Bring your designs to life using real content (or your own!) with this collection of useful plugins

title: Keeping your data in Figma safe and secure: SOC 2 Type 2, SSO, and more
description: At Figma, security is part of everything we do. Learn more about our recent security improvements.

title: How Microsoft built plugins to improve their workflow
description: Discover how Microsoft customizes their processes with Figma Plugins to more seamlessly integrate their Fluent design system into their workflow and help designers be more efficient.

title: From Figma's design team: How to run a design critique
description: Learn six unique methods for design critique used by the Figma design team, along with some tips and best practices for running them effectively.

title: An ode to OpenType: Fall in love with the secret world of fonts
description: In Figma you can now turn on additional ligatures, customize the style of digits, switch to alternative letterforms, and make use of adjustments available in many of today’s fonts.

title: Plugin Show & Tell—Some highlights
description: Get a sneak peak and in-depth look at new and upcoming plugins from 5 plugin developers.

title: How a designer makes background patterns to push her creativity
description: How a designer became a pattern maker through a daily challenge

title: An update on plugin security
description: Learn about Figma's response to the security vulnerabilities that were reported in a 3rd-party library that we use for plugin security

title: Designing for top feature requests, like Smart Animate
description: Introducing Smart Animate and Drag, new prototyping features that will open the door to new transition possibilities in Figma.

title: Customer journey mapping with Figma
description: Learn how to create interactive customer journey maps to better understand your users.

title: Bringing new life to Figma’s brand
description: From typography to colors to illustration, the Figma brand has evolved.

title: How Figma’s multiplayer technology works
description: A peek into the homegrown solution we built as the first design tool with live collaborative editing.

title: Beyond multiplayer: Building community together in Figma
description: Today we’re introducing two new spaces in Figma: The Figma Community and a redesigned Figma workspace.

title: Let’s create Figma’s first user conference together
description: Figma's first user conference will take place on February 6, 2020, in San Francisco, CA. Get involved by submitting our Call for Proposals or filling out our Attendee Application by November 20th.

title: Opening up the data behind your design systems
description: Design System Analytics enables you to see library usage trends, compare libraries, and drill into component usage.

title: Under the hood of Figma’s infrastructure: Here’s what goes into powering a web-based design tool
description: Scaling infrastructure at a fast-growth startup

title: Humanity at work: Gusto brings a new brand to life with Figma
description: Take a look at how Gusto used Figma to create a top-to-bottom brand experience that’s all about people.

title: Design more, resize less, with Auto Layout
description: With Auto Layout buttons can resize with their text. Lists can rearrange themselves when items are moved around. And elements can be nested to create complex interfaces which respond to their content.

title: The decade of design: How the last 10 years transformed design’s role in tech
description: The tech industry, which long exalted the engineer, has finally begun to appreciate the designer too.

title: Made in Figma, 2019: Design by the numbers
description: What you made in Figma, and how you made it, in 2019.

title: Measuring the value of design systems
description: The data science team at Figma set out to find out just how much time (and money) could be saved when teams leverage a design system.

title: Bringing the community together in Figma
description: There is a lot we are excited about as we look to the year ahead. At the core of it all is the community.

title: Figma on Figma: How we built our website design system
description: A look at how the Figma marketing team built, and continues to build, the design system for figma.com

title: Postmortem: Service disruption on January 21-22, 2020
description: The root cause of our recent service outage and our next steps

title: Test designs early and often with the new Maze integration
description: Now user testing is easier and faster with Figma and Maze’s new product integration.

title: New ways to create, collaborate, and share more openly
description: Live from Config 2020, here are all the new features from Figma

title: Reflections on Config, our first user conference
description: When we first set out to organize a user conference, our goal was to create a physical manifestation of the community, a space to have meaningful conversations. Here is a look at some key themes that emerged from those conversations.

title: The Dropbox guide to building creative culture
description: Learn how the design team at Dropbox builds creative culture. Get the Team Values Toolkit, along with tips for running the exercise with your team.

title: Finding confidence in design decisions
description: We have all doubted ourselves and our decisions at some point in our careers. This activity will help guide you through your confidence-building journey.

title: Extend what’s possible with plugins for you and your team
description: Get inspired by plugin creators from Uber, GitHub, and Atlassian—see what's possible and start creating your own plugins.

title: How to run a remote brainstorm
description: The Figma team has been working remotely for a couple weeks now, and we’re still adjusting to our new normal. Here, we share tips for running a remote brainstorm, based on what’s working well for us.

title: How Students Who Design set up Figma in their online classroom
description: In this guide, we’ll walk through how we onboard students, organize our files, teach classes, and provide project feedback with Figma for our online course.

title: Keeping that tight-knit feeling, at a distance
description: It's hard to stay connected while far apart. We've rounded up tips and activities to keep your (currently remote) team feeling like a unit.

title: Building on our open platform: Design in Figma, prototype with Framer
description: Now, you can import your Figma files into Framer Web for more advanced prototyping. Take a look for more on our new integration.

title: Research, remotely
description: There’s a lot that goes into research, and even more to consider when we’re all working from home. Here, we share some tweaks for making remote user interviews and debriefs a little easier.

title: Look no further: New ways to search and provide context in Figma
description: Today, we’re announcing Universal Search and Links in the editor. Read on for more on these new features.

title: Figma + Lambda School: Where the remote classroom comes together to learn
description: Collaborative, interactive lessons inspire a new generation of design professionals

title: 11 activities in Figma for your classroom
description: When learning design, there’s no substitute for hands-on experience. These activities will help your students build and refine their skills.

title: How Inneract Project empowers the next generation of brown and black designers
description: Inneract Project empowers young brown and black students through design education. Here, we share some of their stories.

title: The case for lightweight prototyping
description: Prototypes don't have to be perfect. Read on for more on unpolished prototypes, and some ways the team at Figma is using them.

title: Bringing Figma to even more classrooms
description: We’re expanding the Figma for Education program to include online courses, bootcamps, and school-sponsored hackathons. Read on for more about our announcement.

title: Bridge design and development with the new Zeplin integration
description: Today, we’re introducing a redesigned Zeplin integration with Figma. Check out the new plugin and read on for more details.

title: Figma’s Series D round
description: Figma announces Series D funding led by Peter Levine and Marc Andreessen at a16z.

title: How our product design team works
description: A deep dive into our product design team's day-to-day: meeting cadences, rituals, and culture-building exercises that help everyone stay connected and efficient.

title: How to pair design (and convince your boss to invest in it)
description: Mailchimp's Nina Mehta believes that design pairing can improve work quality and save time. Here, she shares how to structure it, plus tips for convincing your boss that it's a good idea.

title: Finding design inspiration at home
description: Members of the Figma design team share the places they turn to when they need a little spark of creativity.

title: Bringing live Figma embeds to your team’s favorite tools
description: Now, you can embed private Figma files to the tools your team relies on most. Try out embeds in Notion, Dropbox Paper, Jira, and more.

title: The making of Maker Week
description: Our first fully remote Maker Week is behind us, and we’re sharing what it took to bring it to life: the theme, the creative direction, and how we moved our most analog company tradition online.

title: Beyond mocks: co-designing journeys, diagrams, and more
description: A look inside how Segment’s product design team co-designs with researchers, product managers, and engineers.

title: How to get closer with your developer
description: Bringing engineers into the product design process early can improve collaboration and help you get to solutions faster. Read on for three tips from a product designer at Coda.

title: How to evaluate design tools
description: Finding the right tools for your team can be overwhelming. Here, we share a framework for project managing, testing, and choosing a design tool, based on what we’ve heard from our users.

title: How Clearstep simplifies the experience of finding care online
description: Clearstep wanted to bring an intuitive, user-friendly experience to healthcare. Here, they share how they built their symptom checking and care routing platform, from building user flows to managing custom branding.

title: How BT, a 174-year-old company, continues to push the telecommunication industry forward
description: Learn how designers, product owners, content designers, and engineers all collaborate in Figma to drive innovation.

title: A deep dive on deep search
description: We recently rolled out deep search to help you find the right file even faster. Here, the team who built it gives you a behind-the-scenes look at the technical challenges they faced and the tradeoffs they made along the way.

title: Behind the feature: shedding light on shadow spread
description: An engineer shares how we built support for shadow spread in Figma: exploring algorithms, going down W3C spec rabbit holes, and how she made hard decisions about a (seemingly) simple user request.

title: Announcing Config Europe: a call for speakers
description: On September 17, we’re coming together at Config Europe, a virtual conference. Complete our call for speakers form by Friday, 31 July to propose a session.

title: Reflections on a remote internship
description: In this article, Jenning gives us a look into her time at Figma. She shares her expectations, what it’s like to intern remotely, and how she managed updates to Figma’s style picker, inception to launch.

title: Explore the Figma Community
description: As the Figma Community starts rolling out to all Figma users, six creators share the inspiration behind their files—everything from finding a creative outlet during Covid-19 to adapting to a digital classroom.

title: How work is changing at Figma
description: Figma is moving to a hybrid model, allowing both in-person and remote work options. We're sharing our approach, with the hope of helping others think through it.

title: An open letter about my gap year
description: Student Fellow Abigail Africa explores what it means to go “back to school” this fall. In her letter, she shares advice from mentors, reflects on the value of education, and invites other students to join the conversation.

title: How we use Figma templates to facilitate collaboration at Shopify
description: The team at Shopify shares a framework for managing projects in Figma, including a template you can use to collaboration across your organization.

title: React at 60fps: improving scrolling comments in Figma
description: We recently improved FPS performance three-fold for scrolling comments in the Figma canvas. Kiko shares the technical details: how she investigated slow performance, prevented unnecessary re-rendering, and optimized component transformation.

title: Enterprise, explained
description: The enterprise product team at Figma recently shipped updates to help Admins more efficiently manage their Figma Organization. Aashima shares more about this project and how we approach building for organizations of all sizes.

title: Creating multi-brand design systems
description: Leaders from Shopify, Condé Nast, and Harry’s share their learnings about creating and maintaining design systems.
